ogTitle: Wardrop Valley vs Wards River: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wardrop Valley and Wards River.
heading: Wardrop Valley vs Wards River — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wardrop Valley, New South Wales and Wards River, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wardrop Valley has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Wards River (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wardrop Valley or Wards River safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wardrop Valley has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Wards River scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wardrop Valley scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Wards River scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wardrop Valley or Wards River?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wardrop Valley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
